reasoning: Cisneros explicitly supports a public option alongside private insurance. He stated he will fight for the public option originally intended to be part of the ACA and give people the option to buy into Medicare. His Dec 2025 Health Care Action Plan with the New Democrat Coalition focuses on protecting and expanding the ACA and lowering drug costs through government negotiation. He voted for HR 3 (Lower Drug Costs Now Act 2019).

Cisneros earned a 100% rating from Planned Parenthood Action Fund and is a member of the Pro-Choice Caucus. He opposed the Hyde Amendment banning federal funding for abortions and supported repeal of the Helms Amendment barring U.S. foreign aid for abortions. As Under Secretary of Defense he expanded abortion access for military personnel even in states that banned it. His Health Care Action Plan calls for protecting access to abortion and family planning services.

Cisneros introduced the Cut the Burden Keep the Benefits Act (Dec 2025) addressing the burden of tariffs on small businesses. As a New Democrat Coalition member he aligns with a generally pro-trade stance but supports targeted measures. His campaign focuses on bringing manufacturing jobs home while opposing corporate tax breaks for offshoring. He signals opposition to broad protectionist tariff policies without calling for eliminating all tariffs.

Cisneros advocates reducing taxes for working families while eliminating tax breaks for special interests and big corporations that send jobs overseas. In 2019 he introduced a bipartisan bill to increase the SALT deduction cap. He voted against the Republican tax bill. His emphasis is on middle-class tax relief combined with higher corporate and special-interest taxation.

Cisneros co-sponsored the Equality Act (H.R. 5 116th Congress) prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity in employment housing and public accommodations. He co-sponsored H.Res.1209 supporting Supreme Court decisions affirming same-sex marriage. He is a member of the LGBT Equality Caucus and promoted LGBTQ equity as Under Secretary of Defense.

Cisneros co-sponsored the Equality Act which explicitly limits religious exemptions by narrowing the scope of the Religious Freedom Restoration Act (RFRA) when it comes to denying services based on sexual orientation or gender identity. This places religious liberty as important but subordinate to anti-discrimination protections when the two conflict.

No specific sports policy statement found. However Cisneros is described as a fierce advocate for LGBTQ+ and trans rights on his campaign site. He co-sponsored the Equality Act extending civil rights protections to gender identity. He opposed the ban on transgender military service and implemented the policy allowing transgender service members to serve openly at the Pentagon. Rating is based on broader trans-rights advocacy pattern rather than a specific sports policy.

Cisneros supports Ukrainian democracy and opposing Russia's expansion by force. He stated the U.S. must continue to strengthen its international coalition and reaffirm commitment to NATO improve intelligence sharing with allies and strangle terrorist financial networks. His military background and strong NATO commitment align with continued robust support for Ukraine.

Cisneros supports a public option giving people the option to buy into Medicare effectively expanding access beyond current eligibility. His Health Care Action Plan calls for protecting and strengthening Medicare and Medicaid as cornerstones of American health and financial security. He hosted a Medicare town hall and pledged to oppose efforts to cut or privatize Medicare. He supports closing Medicaid coverage gaps.

Cisneros earned a 98% lifetime LCV score and supports ending reliance on polluting fossil fuels by investing in renewable energy. His platform opposes new drilling off coastlines and in the Arctic and supports phasing down extraction on public lands eliminating fossil fuel subsidies pricing carbon emissions and closing the Halliburton loophole on fracking. He pledged not to take money from big oil.

Cisneros co-sponsored the For the People Act (H.R. 1 116th Congress) which includes automatic voter registration expanded early voting and mail-in voting provisions. He called it one of the largest anti-corruption bills of the modern era and voted for it when it passed the House 234-193 in March 2019. The bill expands voter access broadly though does not include online voting.

Cisneros introduced the PROTECT Military Families Act (Jan 2026) to prevent deportation of military families. He fought to protect DREAMers and DACA and supports comprehensive immigration reform including a pathway to citizenship for long-term undocumented residents. He supports protecting TPS recipients keeping families together and enhancing border security in a responsible and humane manner. He voted for the American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6).

Cisneros pledges to stand as a bulwark against any attempts to cut or privatize Social Security calling these programs essential cornerstones of the social safety net. He hosted a town hall specifically on protecting Medicare Medicaid and Social Security. While he has not specifically called for removing the income cap his strong defense of the program combined with support for raising taxes on the wealthy places him at a 2.

Cisneros believes urgent action is needed on climate change. He supports investments in clean energy to create millions of union jobs ending reliance on fossil fuels through wind and solar investment carbon pricing phasing down fossil fuel extraction on public lands and a Buy Clean and Buy America standard. His 98% LCV score reflects strong environmental voting. He supports retraining fossil fuel workers for new energy jobs.

Cisneros co-sponsored the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act the End Racial Profiling Act the Eric Garner Excessive Use of Force Prevention Act and multiple other police reform bills. He served as the Pentagon's first Chief Diversity and Inclusion Officer. He co-sponsored the Equality Act and a resolution condemning police brutality and racial profiling.

In a 2024 election interview Cisneros stated that the focus must be on providing housing for individuals to give them the best chance at receiving support. His overall emphasis on affordable care working families and the social safety net supports expanded housing assistance. Direct housing policy evidence is limited compared to other issues.

Cisneros supports a Constitutional amendment to overturn the Citizens United decision. He calls for increased transparency and an end to dark money in politics by requiring full disclosure of contributors to political committees. He co-sponsored the For the People Act (H.R. 1) which includes small-dollar matching funds and comprehensive campaign finance reform provisions.

Cisneros supports comprehensive immigration reform including the DREAM Act pathways to citizenship for long-term undocumented residents and protecting DREAMers DACA and TPS recipients. He introduced the PROTECT Military Families Act to prevent deportation of military families and voted for H.R. 1044 (Fairness for High-Skilled Immigrants Act). He supports enhancing border security in a responsible and humane manner and a fair and inclusive immigration system.

Cisneros co-sponsored the For the People Act (H.R. 1) which requires states to use independent redistricting commissions for congressional redistricting removing the process from partisan state legislatures. The provisions call for independent commissions with citizen members and prohibit elected officials from serving on them.

Friedman co-authored SB 562 (The Healthy California Act) and AB 1400 (California Guaranteed Health Care for All Act) both single-payer healthcare measures in the CA Assembly. She served on the Assembly Select Committee on Healthcare Delivery Systems and Universal Coverage as a vocal proponent of universal healthcare. Her campaign website identifies her as a Universal Healthcare Advocate. In Congress she fought against Medicaid cuts in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

Friedman co-authored SCA 10 the California constitutional amendment protecting the right to choose abortion and contraceptives which voters approved. She co-authored AB 1242 prohibiting CA law enforcement from cooperating with out-of-state entities on lawful abortion. She joined an amicus brief supporting Planned Parenthood and is endorsed by EMILY's List as a longtime champion of reproductive freedom. She has fought Trump administration efforts to defund Planned Parenthood.

Friedman cosponsored H.R. 7615 to refund tariffs collected under IEEPA and H.R. 2665 (Trade Review Act) requiring independent review before new tariffs are imposed. She rallied with seniors opposing Trump tariffs calling them threats that raise everyday costs. Her office frames tariffs as politically motivated trade disruptions. She favors reining in tariff authority but has not advocated eliminating all tariffs.

In 2019 Friedman voted for a bill to increase income taxes on California residents earning over $1 million per year. She opposed the Trump SALT deduction cap and has advocated for raising or eliminating the cap entirely. She supports adding a rental tax deduction and expanding housing-related tax benefits for lower-income residents while significantly raising taxes on the wealthy.

Friedman authored AB 2663 ensuring every domestic partnership has equal access to full and equal benefits including property tax exclusions. She co-authored SB 179 allowing a third gender option on state documents. She fights LGBTQ+ job and housing discrimination and hosted a congressional LGBTQ roundtable fighting Trump anti-LGBTQ policies. She supported legislation prohibiting advertising conversion therapy.

Friedman's extensive record supporting anti-discrimination protections for LGBTQ+ individuals indicates she prioritizes anti-discrimination over religious exemptions. She supported SB 107 protecting gender-affirming healthcare from out-of-state legal action including from states using religious-freedom arguments. She has consistently voted against exemptions that could override anti-discrimination protections.

No direct trans-athlete sports policy statement found. However her extensive pro-trans record includes supporting SB 107 protecting gender-affirming care supporting housing transgender inmates by gender identity supporting nonbinary legal recognition and fighting Trump attacks on trans rights. California law AB 1266 allows trans students to participate in sports consistent with gender identity and Friedman has never opposed it. Rating based on broader trans advocacy pattern.

Friedman would support additional military aid to Ukraine according to Meet the Freshmen reporting. She supports continued U.S. engagement and international cooperation. She cosponsored H.Res. 68 opposing withdrawal from the Paris Agreement demonstrating commitment to international agreements and alliances.

Friedman co-authored SB 562 and AB 1400 both seeking to establish single-payer healthcare covering all Californians. She served on the Assembly Select Committee on Healthcare Delivery Systems and Universal Coverage as a vocal proponent of universal healthcare. In Congress she fought against massive Medicaid cuts in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act and joined amicus briefs protecting Medicaid patients right to choose providers.

Friedman pledged to focus on phasing out fossil fuel production and incentivizing renewable energy infrastructure pursuing rapid decarbonization. She pledged to oppose any new offshore drilling or fracking. In the CA Assembly she authored clean energy bills voted to oppose offshore drilling (AJR 29) supported extending cap-and-trade (AB 398) and supported the California Climate Crisis Act (AB 1279) codifying carbon neutrality by 2045.

In the CA Assembly Friedman supported legislation to authorize vote-by-mail absentee ballots allow same-day voter registration allow parolees to vote and establish an independent Citizens Redistricting Commission. These positions including automatic and same-day registration expanded mail voting and felon re-enfranchisement align with maximum expansion of voting access.

Friedman explicitly stated she does not support mass deportations saying they would traumatize communities and devastate the local economy. She cosponsored H.R. 2129 (No Round Up Act) restoring civil rights protections to immigration enforcement and the Drain ICE Act. She supported CA sanctuary legislation. She also stated the need to secure the border against violent criminals and close cartel loopholes.

Friedman led a rally at the Hollywood Social Security Office calling out threats from the Trump administration to Social Security access. She fought against cuts and protested Trump efforts to shut down Social Security field offices. Her 99/100 progressive Courage Score and CPC PAC endorsement are consistent with expanding rather than cutting benefits though she has not specified removing the income cap.

Friedman stated she is extremely concerned by the rise of misinformation and the impacts that AI and deepfakes are having on democracy and supports strong regulation around AI. She supported California AB 2655 (Defending Democracy from Deepfake Deception Act 2024) requiring platforms to block deceptive election-related content and pledged to actively work for a similar federal law.

Friedman calls climate change an existential threat and has been described as uncompromising in fighting it. She pledged rapid decarbonization of the economy and phasing out fossil fuel production. In the CA Assembly she supported the California Climate Crisis Act (AB 1279 carbon neutrality by 2045 with 85% direct emissions reductions) cap-and-trade and increased renewable energy targets. Climate and environmental justice are her primary motivations for running for Congress.

Friedman identifies civil rights protections as a core campaign issue. She supported numerous police accountability measures in the CA Assembly including prohibiting certain restraint techniques banning tear gas on protesters expanding public access to police records establishing Sheriff Oversight Boards prohibiting officer transfers following misconduct and requiring AG investigation of police deadly force incidents.

Friedman authored AB 2097 eliminating minimum parking requirements near transit (signed into law 2022) and ADU legislation in the CA Assembly. In Congress she introduced the Cut Red Tape for Housing Act and Build HUBS Act. She advocates for protecting the National Housing Trust Fund expanding Section 8 and increasing Housing Choice Voucher funding. Housing affordability is her most urgent priority for Los Angeles.

Friedman's campaign is explicitly not funded by police fossil fuel real estate or corporate PAC interests. She is endorsed by the Congressional Progressive Caucus which supports overturning Citizens United and public financing of elections. Her strong ethics stance includes calling for independent ethics investigation of Supreme Court justices and higher gift disclosure standards.

Friedman supports DREAMers and a permanent pathway to citizenship. She supports well-funded asylum processes and more supportive policies for refugees. In the CA Assembly she voted for legislation expanding Medi-Cal to immigrants regardless of status authorizing Medicaid for undocumented families prohibiting prisons from working with ICE and establishing sanctuary policies. She also acknowledges the need to secure the border against violent criminals.

Friedman stated she supports much greater accountability by social media companies and strong regulation around AI. She supported AB 2655 (Defending Democracy from Deepfake Deception Act) requiring platforms to block deceptive election-related content. She pledged to actively work for a similar federal law. Her approach emphasizes platform accountability and content verification particularly around elections and AI-generated content.

In the CA Assembly Friedman supported legislation to establish an independent Citizens Redistricting Commission in Sacramento County. California already uses a statewide independent commission removing redistricting from the legislature. Her support for expanding this model to local jurisdictions indicates clear support for independent citizen commissions without elected officials involved in the process.

Garcia is an explicit vocal supporter of Medicare for All. He co-chaired Mayors for Medicare for All with Oakland Mayor Libby Schaaf in 2020. He cosponsored H.R. 3421 the Medicare for All Act in the 118th Congress (May 2023). His official House website states Congress must pass Medicare for All and his campaign says healthcare is a human right and coverage should not depend on employment.

Garcia's official position states every American should be able to safely get an abortion when they need one and he wants to enshrine the right to abortion into federal law. In the 119th Congress he cosponsored H.R. 4611 (affordable abortion coverage) H.R. 12 (limit abortion restrictions) H.R. 4099 (interstate abortion services) and H.R. 2029 (medication abortion access). He is endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund.

As co-chair of the Congressional PORTS Caucus and former overseer of the Port of Long Beach (America's second-largest container port) Garcia is strongly pro-trade. He condemned Trump tariffs in Feb 2026 calling them reckless policies that raised prices and impacted ports. He hosted a press conference about tariff damage. His orientation favors reducing tariffs while his environmental advocacy suggests retaining some environmental trade provisions.

Garcia is a Congressional Progressive Caucus member whose platform states generational cycles of poverty and consolidation of wealth into the hands of a powerful few must end. He advocates expanding the earned income tax credit and fighting for a $15 minimum wage. He launched a guaranteed income pilot as mayor of Long Beach. He supports expanding Medicare Social Security and other programs requiring substantial revenue from higher earners.

Garcia is openly gay and Co-Chair of the Congressional Equality Caucus. He celebrated the signing of the Respect for Marriage Act. He co-introduced the Equality Act providing comprehensive non-discrimination protections for LGBTQ+ people. He sponsored the International Human Rights Defense Act to protect LGBTQ+ rights globally. As mayor Long Beach received a perfect HRC score every year.

Garcia co-chairs the Equality Caucus and cosponsored the Equality Act which prohibits using RFRA as a defense against anti-discrimination claims. He believes anti-discrimination protections should take precedence over religious exemption claims. He identifies as Catholic and has not advocated eliminating all religious practices focusing specifically on preventing religious freedom from justifying discrimination against LGBTQ+ people.

While no specific sports policy statement exists Garcia has the strongest trans advocacy record among these representatives. He is openly LGBTQ co-chairs the Equality Caucus and co-introduced the Equality Act prohibiting gender identity discrimination in all public settings. He forcefully condemned targeting of trans elected officials and stated government has a responsibility to protect the LGBTQ+ community especially transgender and nonbinary Americans. The Equality Act would cover sports as public accommodations.

Garcia voted in favor of all three military aid package supplementals for Ukraine Israel and Taiwan in April 2024 along with most Democrats. He has not publicly pushed for significantly increasing aid beyond current levels nor called for reducing it. His voting pattern aligns with continuing current support levels for Ukraine.

Garcia explicitly supports Medicare for All and was co-chair of Mayors for Medicare for All. He cosponsored H.R. 3421 (Medicare for All Act) in the 118th Congress. His congressional website states he wants to expand benefits so every American has access to dental vision and preventative care.

Garcia supports the Green New Deal and signed the No Fossil Fuel Money pledge. He introduced the BUILD GREEN Infrastructure and Jobs Act with Sen. Warren authorizing $500 billion for transportation electrification. As mayor Long Beach passed an aggressive climate action plan to end reliance on fossil fuels. He introduced the Clean Shipping Act to reduce shipping pollution. His approach favors phasing out fossil fuels through transition rather than an immediate ban.

Garcia explicitly supports the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act and the Freedom to Vote Act. He cosponsored H.R. 7265 (Vote by Mail Tracking Act) in the 119th Congress. The Freedom to Vote Act includes automatic voter registration expanded early and mail-in voting and election security provisions.

Garcia called for abolishing ICE in January 2026. As mayor he made Long Beach a Sanctuary City with protections for undocumented residents. He reintroduced the ICE Security Reform Act and led congressional field hearings on mass deportation in Los Angeles. He traveled to El Salvador to advocate for wrongfully deported individuals and launched an Oversight Immigration Enforcement Dashboard to document ICE misconduct.

Garcia pledges to fight to protect and expand eligibility and benefits for Social Security. He piloted a guaranteed income program as mayor of Long Beach. As a Congressional Progressive Caucus member he aligns with the CPC Social Security 2100 agenda which includes expanding benefits and raising the payroll tax cap.

Garcia is a strong Green New Deal supporter who views climate change as an existential threat. He introduced the BUILD GREEN Act authorizing $500 billion for transportation electrification over 10 years. As mayor he signed the Global Covenant of Mayors developed Long Beach's first Climate Action and Adaptation Plan banned Styrofoam plastic straws and plastic bags. His Clean Shipping Act addresses port pollution. He favors rapid transition through investment and job creation.

Garcia states systemic racism is steeped in our institutions. As mayor he launched an Office of Equity diversified leadership and integrated My Brother's Keeper programs. He supports the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act and pledges to modernize and expand anti-discrimination laws. He cosponsored the Equality Act and led 49 colleagues pushing Fortune 1000 companies to maintain workplace DEI commitments.

Garcia calls housing a Human Right and advocates for expanding affordable housing and building quality public housing. He co-founded and co-chairs the bipartisan Congressional YIMBY Caucus focused on increasing housing supply by removing barriers to construction. His approach combines building more units mixed-use zoning eliminating parking minimums and financial incentives for construction near transit and urban job centers.

Garcia explicitly supports ending Citizens United. His website has a dedicated page titled Fighting to Get Big Money out of Politics. He cosponsored the Stop Ballroom Bribery Act (Nov 2025) to prevent presidential corruption through private donations. He signed the No Fossil Fuel Money pledge refusing contributions from fossil fuel companies.

Garcia himself was an undocumented immigrant who benefited from the 1986 immigration reform. He wants every undocumented kid and their family to have the ability to earn citizenship. As mayor he made Long Beach a Sanctuary City and established a Justice Fund for families facing deportation. He called for abolishing ICE and cosponsored the Southeast Asian Deportation Relief Act. He does not advocate open borders but focuses on expanded pathways and protections.

As mayor of Long Beach Garcia proposed ballot initiatives including creating redistricting commissions which passed. He supported Prop 50 in California to redraw congressional maps. California uses an independent citizens redistricting commission and Garcia has consistently supported this model both locally and at the state level removing elected officials from the process.

Gomez is a founding member of the Medicare for All Caucus and has cosponsored the Medicare for All Act since 2017 including H.R. 3514 in the 118th Congress. He authored the Choose Medicare Act (H.R. 3911) creating a Medicare Part E public option as a stepping stone toward universal coverage. He explicitly calls Medicare for All the most effective way to expand healthcare and reduce costs.

Gomez has a 100% rating from NARAL Pro-Choice America and an F grade from Susan B. Anthony List. He cosponsored and voted to pass the Women's Health Protection Act to codify Roe v. Wade. He is an original cosponsor of the Ensuring Women's Right to Reproductive Freedom Act and the Reproductive Health Travel Fund Act. He received the Champion of Choice Award from Planned Parenthood and authored CA legislation expanding abortion access in rural communities.

Gomez focused on strengthening worker rights and labor provisions in trade agreements specifically the USMCA. He wrote to the Biden administration about unfair labor practices emphasizing protecting workers on both sides of the border. He was considered for U.S. Trade Representative suggesting mainstream Democratic trade views. His progressive orientation favors reduced tariffs with strong labor and environmental protections rather than broad protectionism or pure free trade.

Gomez sponsored the For the 99.8% Act and For the 99.5% Act with Sen. Sanders to strengthen the estate tax on ultra-wealthy families raising trillions from billionaire families. He voted against the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act. In 2025 during Ways and Means Committee markup he proposed an amendment to tax billionaires that Republicans blocked. He stated ultra-rich families have used the tax code to acquire mass wealth while working Americans fall further behind.

Gomez voted for the Respect for Marriage Act repealing DOMA and requiring federal and state recognition of same-sex marriages. He has a 100% HRC score for both the 115th and 116th Congresses. He is a member of the Congressional LGBTQ Equality Caucus and voted for the Equality Act. He secured federal funding for transgender workforce training and modernized birth certificates for same-sex parents.

Gomez voted for the Equality Act which explicitly prioritizes anti-discrimination protections for LGBTQ individuals over religious exemptions — a bill opposed by many religious organizations for lacking religious freedom carve-outs. He supports the Prohibition of Medicaid Funding for Conversion Therapy Act. His consistent 100% HRC score shows he prioritizes civil rights over religious exemptions when the two conflict though he has not called for eliminating all religious exemptions.

No specific trans athlete sports statement found. However Gomez voted for the Equality Act prohibiting discrimination based on gender identity. He is a member of the LGBTQ Equality Caucus has a 100% HRC score and secured funding for transgender workforce programs. He voted against the NDAA citing provisions discriminating against LGBTQ+ military members. Rating is based on broader trans-rights advocacy pattern rather than specific sports policy.

Gomez voted against the $40 billion Ukraine Military Aid supplemental in 2022 alongside progressive Democrats reflecting concerns about oversight of massive military spending rather than opposition to Ukraine itself. However he led 98 members of Congress urging TPS designation for Ukraine and applauded when granted. His overall stance supports Ukraine through humanitarian and diplomatic channels while being cautious about blank-check military spending.

Gomez is a founding member of the Medicare for All Caucus and has cosponsored the Medicare for All Act in every Congress since 2017. He authored the Choose Medicare Act (H.R. 3911) creating a new Medicare Part E open to all Americans and businesses. He receives 100% ratings from the Alliance for Retired Americans and the National Committee to Preserve Social Security and Medicare.

Gomez is an original cosponsor of the Green New Deal and the Climate Emergency Resolution. He sponsored the Climate Solutions Act of 2019 transitioning to 100% renewable energy by 2035. He cosponsored the No Drilling in Our Backyards Act. He has a 98% lifetime LCV score and authored key climate provisions in the Inflation Reduction Act including EV tax credits and home energy efficiency credits. His timeline of phasing out fossil fuels by 2035 is ambitious but not an immediate ban.

Gomez is an original cosponsor of and voted to pass the For the People Act of 2021 (H.R. 1) which includes automatic voter registration online voter registration same-day registration expanded early voting with a 15-day minimum expanded vote-by-mail elimination of partisan gerrymandering through independent commissions and public campaign financing. He supported it in both the 116th and 117th Congresses.

Gomez has never voted for a standalone appropriations bill that funds ICE. He cosponsors the U.S. Citizenship Act providing pathways to citizenship for millions of undocumented immigrants. He was one of only 19 Democrats who voted against border wall funding. He sought to redirect $175 billion from ICE and CBP to housing. He inspected a downtown LA ICE facility in 2026 to check for rights violations.

Gomez cosponsored the Social Security 2100 Act (H.R. 860) and Save our Social Security Now Act (H.R. 8171) both of which expand benefits and ensure long-term solvency primarily through raising or eliminating the income cap on payroll taxes. He receives 100% ratings from the Alliance for Retired Americans and National Committee to Preserve Social Security and Medicare. He held a Social Security Town Hall in 2025 stating cutting benefits is simply not an option.

Gomez scrutinized Instacart AI-powered dynamic pricing practices and raised concerns about facial recognition technology discriminating against people of color after being personally misidentified by Amazon AI facial recognition as a suspected criminal. He wrote to Amazon three times demanding accountability. He cosponsored legislation related to algorithmic transparency and consumer protection. His concerns center on AI harms requiring government monitoring and approval before deployment.

Gomez is an original cosponsor of the Green New Deal with AOC — a plan to decarbonize the U.S. economy within ten years. He sponsored the Climate Solutions Act of 2019 to transition to 100% renewable energy by 2035. He is an original cosponsor of the Climate Emergency Resolution declaring climate change an emergency. He helped pass the Inflation Reduction Act the largest climate investment in U.S. history. His 98% lifetime LCV score reflects consistent pro-environment voting.

Gomez cosponsors the Commission to Study and Develop Reparation Proposals for African Americans Act. He cosponsored and voted for the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act to end qualified immunity ban chokeholds and create a federal registry of police misconduct. He is an original cosponsor of the Ending Qualified Immunity Act. He voted to pass the MORE Act to decriminalize marijuana and address war on drugs harms. He cosponsored the Equality Act and COVID-19 Hate Crimes Act signed into law.

Gomez founded and chairs the Congressional Renters Caucus. He introduced the Make Housing Affordable and Defend Democracy Act (2025) to redirect $175 billion toward housing. He authored the Rent Relief Act the Affordable Housing Equity Act the Revitalization Downtown and Main Streets Act for converting offices to housing and the End Junk Fees for Renters Act. He proposed $50000 downpayment assistance for first-time homebuyers and protested on Capitol steps to extend the eviction moratorium.

Gomez authored the California DISCLOSE Act in the State Assembly to expand campaign transparency. He supports overturning Citizens United with the goal of public financing of elections. He helped pass the For the People Act (H.R. 1) which includes small-donor matching at a 6:1 ratio and limits on dark money. He is a cosponsor of the congressional DISCLOSE Act rejects corporate PAC money and is endorsed by End Citizens United. He sponsored legislation to ban congressional stock trading.

Gomez cosponsors the U.S. Citizenship Act providing pathways to citizenship for millions of undocumented immigrants and increasing legal immigration channels. He supports the Dream and Promise Act for DREAMers and TPS holders. He voted to increase per-country immigration caps (H.R. 1044 passed 365-65). He was one of only 19 Democrats to vote against border wall funding. He led efforts to secure $21.8 million for housing newly arrived migrants. He does not advocate open borders but strongly favors expanded pathways.

Gomez voted for and cosponsored the For the People Act (H.R. 1) which requires states to adopt independent redistricting commissions with 15 members composed of 5 Democrats 5 Republicans and 5 independents for congressional redistricting. The bill includes a statutory ban on partisan gerrymandering and requires maps to achieve majority approval including at least one vote from each party group. This model matches independent commissions with equal party representation.
